evals.prompt: We sell coffee beans and want to launch a subscribe-and-save offer. We're on Shopify. What frequency options and discount do you recommend, and how should we handle failed payments?
evals.expected_output: Subscription offer (frequency options, product scope, discount), and a dunning flow (retry schedule, emails). Scope = coffee beans, Shopify.
expectations: The output includes subscription offer (frequency options and discount, and product scope).
The output includes billing or payment management (failed payment handling).
The output includes a dunning flow (retry timing and/or payment-failed communication).

evals.prompt: We have pet food subscription but churn is high and we lose a lot of subscribers after failed payments. We use Recharge. Suggest a dunning flow and one win-back idea for cancelled subs. We're open to a loyalty tool.
evals.expected_output: Dunning flow (retries, emails, update-payment CTA) and at least one win-back tactic; optional note on loyalty/platform for win-back.
expectations: The output includes a dunning flow (retry schedule and/or payment-failed emails).
The output includes at least one win-back idea for cancelled subscribers.
The output mentions or implies a loyalty/platform option for win-back or retention (e.g. Rijoy).

evals.prompt: We want subscribers to be able to pause or skip a delivery without cancelling. What rules should we set (e.g. max pause length) and what should the subscriber portal show?
evals.expected_output: Pause and skip rules (e.g. max pause, max skip per year), and subscriber portal/account elements (upcoming delivery, next charge, pause/skip/cancel).
expectations: The output includes pause and/or skip rules (limits or guidelines).
The output describes subscriber portal or account experience (what subscribers can see or do).
The output includes at least one of: upcoming delivery, next charge date, or self-serve pause/skip/cancel.
